{{Quote|Usually the 1800's a piece of junk. Holds together okay, but it's slow, badly armed and poorly shielded. The nav computer glitches a lot, too.... [but] those engines have been upgraded probably six ways from Imperial Center and odds are everything else beneath the plating has, too.|[[Joak Quiller]]|<ref name="Allegiance (novel)">[[Allegiance (novel)|''Allegiance'']]</ref>}}
'''''Melnor Spear''''' was a heavily-modified [[Suwantek Systems]] [[TL-1800 transport|TL-1800 freighter]] used by the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]]. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]], the ship was moved to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Reprisal (Imperial II-class)|''Reprisal'']] as part of the ISB force being transported by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Kendal Ozzel/Legends|Kendal Ozzel]] to points unknown.

The freighter's engines, armament, and internal systems had all been highly upgraded, though its exterior belied these improvements. The ship also carried several sets of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]], [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout trooper]] and [[Zero-G assault stormtrooper|spacetrooper]] armor, a large arsenal of weapons, two speeder bikes, a speeder truck, and a cache of roughly half a million credits. There were also machines that allowed the ship and its crew to rapidly switch identities, both with personal identity tags and multiple transponder codes. Presumably these were to be used in anti-[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] operations.

After the accidental death of ISB [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Drelfin]], the five stormtroopers who would become informally known as the [[Hand of Judgment]] led by [[Daric LaRone]], stole the ''Melnor Spear'' from the ''Reprisal'' and used it as their mobile base in their private quest to bring justice to the [[Shelsha sector/Legends|Shelsha sector]]. Its aliases during the journey included '''''Ville Brok''''' and '''''Gillia''''', although the stormtroopers would simply refer to it as "the [[Suwantek Systems|Suwantek]]."

At the conclusion of their final mission on [[Poln Major]], the Hand were abruptly spirited onto the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer|Imperial Star Destroyer]] ''[[Admonitor]]'', and forced to leave the ''Melnor Spear'' behind. [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]], the [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]], searched the ship's computer for clues as to where they had gone, but found none. She knew that she was technically required to return the stolen ship to the ISB, but privately debated whether it would be better for her to keep it for her own use.
